channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
item.description: [BBC Technology] It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

item.title: AI content can rank, but quality still decides winners
item.description: [MarTech] Semrush’s study shows AI content is already competing in search, but performance still comes down to execution, not the tool. The post AI content can rank, but quality still decides winners appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Ex-Meta worker investigated for downloading 30,000 private Facebook photos
item.description: [BBC Technology] The former employee was fired from Meta after being suspected of downloading 30,000 photos, the company said.

item.title: Anthropic tops US$30 billion run rate, seals deal with Broadcom
item.description: [Business Times] More than 1,000 business customers spend over US$1 million annually on Claude services

item.title: Using AI isn’t the same as getting value from it
item.description: [MarTech] Most teams have access to AI, but still struggle to see meaningful results. The issue isn’t the tools, but where they’re applied. The post Using AI isn’t the same as getting value from it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

item.title: OpenAI pauses UK data centre deal over energy costs and regulation
item.description: [BBC Technology] The project was part of a package of tech investment promising the UK could become an AI superpower.

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] AI marketing is becoming more transactional and citation-driven, with ads, commerce and trust signals converging inside chatbot conversations. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: How to make AI work with context instead of prompts
item.description: [MarTech] AI struggles in enterprises not because of prompts, but because it lacks context. Here's how to build systems that fix that. The post How to make AI work with context instead of prompts appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Time to buy US AI, tech names if Middle East de-escalation holds, say analysts
item.description: [Business Times] Singapore will also be the first to see clear benefits as AI comes to fore in Asia

item.title: From shopping to gaming: ShopBack sees over 30% growth in FY 2026
item.description: [Business Times] This uptick in revenue marks a departure from the flat numbers the company has seen in the past two years

item.title: Fintech player Aspire taps JPMorgan to help companies with forex conversions
item.description: [Business Times] They will initially focus on multiple currencies, including the pound, Singapore and US dollar

item.title: Me and My Money: Growing up in a tea plantation taught him about money
item.description: [Straits Times] The Singapore country manager of MoneyHero Group takes a relaxed approach to his finances.

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

item.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
item.description: [The Verge AI] The illustration for The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a jump scare. Altman stands in a blue sweater with a blank expression. Around his head hovers a cluster of disembodied faces - creepy alt-Altmans, their expressions ranging from anger to open-mouthed woe. Some barely look like

item.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
item.description: [The Verge AI] Two weeks ago, I was getting ready to log off work when I got a text message. "Oh wow, I was checking out Mitski. did you know people are saying her Dad was a CIA operative?" Normally, that kind of out-of-the-blue text from a friend wouldn't faze me. This time, my eyes bugged. The unprompted [&#8230

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: [The Verge AI] In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with video after video of what was happening on the ground: Explosions over Tehran. Smoke billowing in the sky.

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: [WIRED AI] Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.

item.title: 滴滴自动驾驶张博：深耕AI、硬件、场景三大能力，持续强化创新突破
item.description: [钛媒体] 滴滴自动驾驶始终坚持负责任的科技创新。

item.title: Cathay Pacific to cut flights from mid-May to end-June as jet fuel prices surge
item.description: [CNA Tech] The Hong Kong-based airline will cancel about 2 per cent of its scheduled passenger flights from May 16 to Jun 30, 2026.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: [WIRED AI] From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

item.title: 所谓Skill，不过是AI时代的工业垃圾
item.description: [钛媒体] 所谓Skill，不过是AI时代的工业垃圾

item.title: 奥特曼家差点被烧，AI争议滑向物理暴力
item.description: [钛媒体] “我懂你的难处，但你烧我家是否有点过分”

item.title: 大模型厂商，是时候告别Token狂欢了
item.description: [钛媒体] 更便宜的Token不是未来。

item.title: 拿下豪华种子轮，一家明星AI公司宣布倒闭
item.description: [36氪] 作者/吴琼 报道/投资界PEdaily 唏嘘一幕。 近日，知名AI创业公司Yupp宣布：将停止服务，于4月15日正式关闭。要知道，这距离Yupp产品上线才不到一年时间。 曾经，Yupp的故事颇具前景：瞄准AI模型评测赛道——通过免费模型服务吸引用户评测，再将测评数据卖给模型厂商。犹记得2024年，公司拿下3300万美元（约合2.2亿元）的豪华种子轮，身后聚集a16z合伙人、Google首席科学家、Twitter联合创始人等超45位天使投资人。 只是，Yupp却以极为荒诞的方式失败：投资人的钱还没花完，AI技术的演进就让其市场不复存在。而这一切，就发生在短短几个月之间。

item.title: 奥特曼家被炸了！
item.description: [36氪] 奥特曼家被炸了 【导读】当地时间周五清晨，奥特曼家被炸了。奥特曼发出家人和孩子的照片，并且发出长文表示，AGI如今已经如同魔戒一般，让人做出疯狂的举动。 奥特曼家被炸了！ 就在当地时间周五清晨，一名20岁男子向奥特曼旧金山中投掷了燃烧弹，引起火灾。 这栋豪宅价值2700万美元。 凌晨4点12分，警方接到报警，调查后发现无人受伤，火势已被控制。但嫌疑人已经徒步逃跑，体貌特征已被通报。 凌晨5点07分左右，警方又接到报警称，OpenAI办公楼外，一名身份不明的男子扬言要烧毁大楼。 警员很快发现，该男子与向奥特曼家中投掷燃烧弹的嫌疑人特征相符，应为同一人，警方立即将其拘留

item.title: Japan approves additional $4 billion for chipmaker Rapidus
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: DeepSeek looks for data centre engineers in Inner Mongolia
item.description: [Business Times] It is the first time the company has publicly disclosed a data centre location

item.title: 星际之门三高管同时出走，奥特曼屋漏偏逢连夜雨
item.description: [钛媒体] 奥特曼的AI基建牌桌还稳吗？

item.title: 专治AI说谎，25岁天才少女公司估值过百亿
item.description: [钛媒体] 硅谷新贵，窄路逆袭

item.title: 雷军投的"红娘"要去IPO了
item.description: [36氪] 伊达母公司米连科技向港交所递交招股书 作者 | 王涛编辑 | 吾人 来源 | 融中财经 又一个“红娘平台”要去IPO了。 前不久，伊达母公司米连科技向港交所递交招股书，由华泰国际担任独家保荐人。这也是继去年9月米连首次递表失效后的第二轮冲刺。 据悉，米连在海内外运营有多款在线情感社交平台。其中，2017年上线的伊对APP是米连科技的核心产品，更新后的招股书显示，其开创的主持人用户引导的三方互动，以及收入分成模式，在2025年共计为母公司带来了33.67亿元营收，占总营收比例约八成。 与互联网企业常见的“赔本赚吆喝”的套路不同，米连的财报十分亮眼。招股书披露其在

item.title: First-class seats drive airline profits. It wasn’t always this way
item.description: [Straits Times] Airlines became better at selling premium seats to travellers, and customers grew much more willing to pay for them.

item.title: 低空经济第一股，来了
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨韦香惠 来源丨东四十条资本 拿下开年低空经济最大一笔融资后，仅隔不到两个月，沃飞长空便迅速启动IPO进程，直指A股“低空经济第一股”。 就在签署上市辅导协议前一天，公司完成股份制改造，由“有限责任公司”整体变更为“股份有限公司”，注册资本由约1.77亿元提升至3.6亿元；与此同时，吉利科技集团CEO徐志豪接任董事长，标志着这家吉利系低空企业在治理结构与战略层面进一步向集团化、产业化靠拢。 从融资节奏来看，沃飞长空已累计完成五轮市场化融资，最新一轮金额近10亿元，刷新2026年开年以来低空经济领域最大单笔融资纪录。但在资本加速涌入的同时，公司核心机型AE200仍处于适航审定

item.title: OpenAI identifies security issue involving third-party tool, says user data was not accessed
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Claude新模型危险，鲍威尔召集华尔街紧急开会！全美安全股暴跌2万亿
item.description:
[36氪] 【导读】刚刚，华尔街巨头被紧急召往华盛顿！Mythos这个模型让美国财长和美联储主席都恐慌了，向全美金融业CEO发出警告。短短一年内，SaaS市场经历了一场浩劫，2万亿美元直接蒸发。 就在这周二，鲍威尔和贝森特紧急召见华尔街，向所有人发出警告—— Anthropic的最新模型Mythos，可能对金融业造成重大风险！ 从蒸发2万亿美元，到紧急召集CEO进华盛顿——Anthropic的AI，正在触碰金融世界最敏感的神经。 就在今天，这个新闻已经在全网刷屏了。
Anthropic
Anthropic 本周二，一场没有提前预告、没有公开议程的会议，在华盛顿财政部

item.title: 36氪首发 | 打破日本工业镜头垄断，松山湖团队融资数千万
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，高端工业镜头研发商——阿基视觉科技（东莞）有限公司（下称“阿基视觉”）近日完成数千万元天使轮融资，由东莞科创、深担创投、松禾资本及林润集团联合投资，德太资本担任本轮融资顾问。本轮融资将用于团队扩充、产能扩建、研发配套及市场拓展，加速高端工业镜头的国产化替代进程。 阿基视觉成立于2022年，总部位于东莞松山湖。2025年，CEO吴选胜加入阿基视觉，吴选胜曾任日本工业镜头龙头Moritex中国区董事长兼总经理，负责技术团队与运营管理，此前还在全球500强捷普电子、欧洲企业XAL及台达电子任职，拥有超十年光学行业经验。 自从吴选胜加入阿基视觉以来，

item.title: US judge blocks Arizona criminal case against Kalshi at CFTC's request
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Tesla's supervised self-driving software gets Dutch okay, first in Europe
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: The risk of relying on spousal maintenance for all expenses after divorce
item.description: [Straits Times] There is nothing stopping retired spouses from asking for a revision or even a revocation due to financial hardship.

item.title: Vance, Bessent questioned tech giants on AI security before Anthropic's Mythos release, CNBC reports
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Wall St ends mixed, notches weekly gains as investors parse Middle East negotiations
item.description: [Straits Times] The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 282.62 points, or 0.59 per cent, to 47,903.18.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: [The Verge AI] San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard reports. The incident was caught on surveillance cameras shortly before 7AM ET. Later that morning, someone mat

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: [WIRED AI] The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

item.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
item.description: [The Verge AI] Donald Trump has spun the recent rescue of a downed airman whose fighter jet was destroyed behind Iranian borders as a resounding success. But the story is very different in one of the many viral, AI-generated Lego videos that have been produced by Iranian content creation group Explosive Media in t

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI i

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: [WIRED AI] An attacker allegedly threw a molotov cocktail at the OpenAI CEO’s residence before making threats outside of the startup’s headquarters.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: [WIRED AI] Onix is launching a “Substack of bots,” where digital twins of health and wellness influencers dispense advice 24/7. And maybe hawk their products.

item.title: Mayor warns of London 'disinformation blizzard'
item.description: [BBC Technology] Sir Sadiq Khan claims London is being targeted with disinformation portraying it as a city "in decline".

item.title: S’pore working to boost liquidity, grow pipeline of IPOs, draw more investors to revive stock market
item.description: [Straits Times] Much work is needed to achieve a “true renaissance”, said MAS assistant managing director Gillian Tan.

item.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: Dancer with MND performs on stage again through digital avatar
item.description: [BBC Technology] Breanna Olson said brainwave tech was able to re-establish the expression and connection she felt had gone.

item.title: Dell: Agents drive more ecommerce traffic, but conversions lag
item.description: [MarTech] Dell sees rising traffic from AI platforms, but the impact remains inconsistent as search continues to drive most ecommerce performance. The post Dell: Agents drive more ecommerce traffic, but conversions lag appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: [The Verge AI] Sam Altman's tenure at OpenAI has been… messy. Messy to the point where Altman was briefly fired from his role as CEO, only to be reinstated days later, at which point he began reshaping the organization permanently. This week, The New Yorker published a deep look at Altman, his time at OpenAI, and

item.title: A 6-step AI workflow for building better seasonal campaigns
item.description: [MarTech] Turn scattered inputs into clear campaign strategy by combining CRM data, research and structured prompting into a repeatable system. The post A 6-step AI workflow for building better seasonal campaigns appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Bessent, Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic model risks, sources say
item.description: [Business Times] The company is stopping short of a broad release, citing concerns of the model exposing cybersecurity vulnerabilities

item.title: AI Podcasters Really Want to Tell You How to Keep a Man Happy
item.description: [WIRED AI] Videos of fake relationship guru podcasters are reinforcing gender tropes and racking up millions of views, all the while driving sales to AI influencer schools.

item.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
item.description: [BBC Technology] The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

item.title: Anthropic’s AI model scare sparks urgent US warning to bank CEOs
item.description: [Straits Times] Mythos can identify and then exploit vulnerabilities in every major operating system and web browser.
